programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nadiya Hussain takes a trip to the Himalayan country of Nepal to learn more about its people, places and food as part of her adventure to explore her identity. She begins in Kathmandu, where she joins a supper club run by young foodies who share with her the vastly varied cuisine of their country, then heads to the historic city of Bhaktapur, which was devastated by the earthquake of 2015, and visits an abbey to meet nuns from a Buddhist sect who practise kung fu and teach female empowerment(n)

They also tackle their own health concerns, from Hugh's stress to Steph's sensitive stomach(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the midweek Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v West Ham United at King Power Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford. Leicester have enjoyed an excellent campaign so far, but the Hammers will have been looking to demonstrate how much they have improved under David Moyes. United have endured an inconsistent campaign and the Clarets will have hoped for a first win here since 1962. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Norwich City, Chelsea v Arsenal, Aston Villa v Watford, Bournemouth v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Southampton, Everton v Newcastle United and Sheffield United v Manchester City(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville investigate the quality of mobile phone coverage and hear about home owners stuck with mortgages they can barely afford(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a property in Stourbridge in the Midlands, a terraced house in Alkrington in Manchester and a semi-detached in Wigan in Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with a woman who planned to move to New Zealand following a painful divorce, discovering if she persuaded her second husband to join her in starting over(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: How a pickpocket prowling railway stations preying on women carrying backpacks and bags was hunted down. Plus, the thieves thwarted from taking a parcel from someone's doorstep(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt are in Lincoln to help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Sheffield, while host Eric Knowles indulges his passion for vintage cars(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie and David's bond grows, while Jimmi receives a visit from his father. Emma finds herself trapped between two feuding brothers - and their cockroaches(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing clergyman uncovers secrets, hypocrisy and murder at a finishing school for young ladies. Mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £550,000 budget look for a property in Dorset, and visits the oyster beds of Poole harbour to learn how the shellfish are farmed sustainably(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth continue their profit-making challenge along the highways and byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Along the way, they visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy more pastry products, before heading off to auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells are thrown into panic as past events come back to haunt them and with Sharon having the upper hand - what lengths will she go to, to get revenge? As the election party gets under way, everyone is concerned by the sight of a drunken Linda, leaving Mick trying desperately to get her home. Lola urges Sheree to make amends with Patrick, but he is not prepared for what she tells him, and Kat opens up to Kush about her past(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: There is great excitement in Saint Marie when the Tour des Antilles cycling race comes to the island but the event turns sinister when the favourite, Xavier Prince, is found dead halfway around. All evidence initially points to Xavier having crashed on one of the most dangerous sections of the course, but Jack suspects it has more to do with the discovery of a piece of fabric torn from a cycling shirt. However, one of the riders is keeping a sensitive secret. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Stoke Newington, Hackney, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and newcomer Steven Moore start their hunt in the Scottish Borders, buying items on their journey to sell at auction in Crooklands, near Kendal in the Lake District(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two farming families hope their much-loved pygmy goats will bring them acclaim at the Mid Devon Show, but the competition is tough and there is a record number of goats in the ring(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace hosts the first round of Knockout week, as the 12 chefs to make it through the finals compete to stay in the contest. The chefs face an invention test in which they must not use any modern kitchen gadgets. The four weakest performing contestants then go through to a cook-off, to decide which two will be eliminated from the competition(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine presents an appeal on behalf of Parkinson's UK, which funds research, support networks and specialised nurses to help those with the condition(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the mixed pairs final and a singles match. Last year saw English pair Robert Paxton and Ellen Falkner defeat Nicky Brett of England and Helen Carman of New Zealand. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Royal Marines Museum in Southsea, Hampshire, Katherine Higgins learns about the local corset industry and Charles Hanson hears all about cap ribbons worn by German navy men. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin hitches a ride on hovercraft to uncover the history of the clever craft that was designed, built, and is still used on the Solent(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England en route to Lincolnshire, discovering how the Crossrail project is helping to conserve birds and taking a gelato cart to Southend(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Holby City stars Rosie Marcel and Bob Barret look for bargains in the Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire flatlands, with the expert advice of David Harper and Louise Gostelow. However, the road trip is jeopardised when a flooded river blocks the way to the antiques shops - but a detour leads Bob to a beloved location, where he learns about landscape gardening(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A brother and sister team fish for oysters in a traditional wooden sailing boat on the Fal estuary - but this is not enough to support them, so now they plan to trade it in for a small trawler. Further down river in St Mawes, a veteran trawler captain reflects on a past brush with death and the changing face of his home town, as working boats give way to yachts(n)

programme.title: Chris Packham: 7.7 Billion People and Counting
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates the impact of a growing human population on the world in light of predictions that the number of humans could reach 10 billion by the year 2050. He considers the steps that will need to be taken for Earth's natural resources to support so many people, visiting a city in Brazil that is on the verge of running out of water and a community school in Nigeria that may present a possible solution. Featuring contributions from leading environmental experts including David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: It is another full house at Sam's, as she cooks risotto for guests invited and uninvited. Phil arrives with her new boyfriend, and Jeff arrives with his new girlfriend and a monkey. Comedy, with Pamela Adlon and guest starring Sharon Stone(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedians Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson parody a 1980s safety video(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to look back at the Conference Championship matches(n)

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents action from the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the mixed pairs final and a singles match. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The keen horticulturalist visits some of the most famous and interesting gardens in the US. He begins his trek in a prairie, the original American flowering wilderness, only to find much of it has disappeared. In New York, he explores vegetables being grown on the city's rooftops, visits an enthusiastic allotment community in the Bronx and learns about the history of America's most famous public green space, Central Park. In Philadelphia, Monty examines one of the country's most famous gardens and enjoys a modern version of a prairie garden(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the case of a poultry farmer who was hanged for the murder of his wife, after her body was found buried under a chicken run(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to hospital with a suspected stroke. Meanwhile, medics and emergency service staff raise money for an MRI scanner for Shetland(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth look for bargains in Scotland, visiting the largest lowland loch and getting a crash course in chemistry before attending an auction at Kinbuck(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers head to Ryedale to show off the finest animals in their flocks, including a retired businessman who is hoping to carry on his wife's legacy(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam explores the rich culture of Scotland, before exploring the history of deaf education in Scotland. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holidaymakers in Greece that left one woman blind. Plus, the internet scams that have left thousands of people out of pocket, and Michelle Ackerley is with experts as they tell members of the public if their precious jewellery is genuine or not(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Includes live coverage of Prime Minister's Questions from midday(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the second round of the open singles and the semi-finals of the ladies singles. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher are in Ushaw in County Durham as they search for antiques to take to auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway journey through 1930s Britain from Canterbury to Skegness reaches Witham in Essex, where he visits the factory of the world's oldest supplier of metal-framed windows, which became popular in the Thirties. Crossing into Suffolk, he alights at Ipswich and discovers the story of a group of child refugees whose history is intertwined with that of Michael's family(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether diet can affect mental health, and Alain Gregoire investigates the future technology that could spot signs of dementia before it develops. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals a new treatment for the skin condition psoriasis, and Guddi Singh finds out the best way to calm a crying baby(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Having followed the wrong boy for years, Aziraphale and Crowley must now try to locate the whereabouts of the real Antichrist. The pair head back to Tadfield, to the old convent and site of the baby-swap, only to discover it no longer exists. As they head back on to the road, they cross paths with Anathema, and soon discover that she has lost Agnes Nutter's book(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Larry Dean and Gary Delaney(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In America, it's an epidemic. Now new evidence from Public Health England raises concern about the UK's use of prescription opioids. Dr Michael Mosley investigates this growing problem, meeting patients struggling with addiction and GPs fighting a constant battle to help those suffering from chronic pain. He also uncovers worrying evidence of people abusing over-the-counter opioids and discovers how easy it is to buy strong opioids online. Michael also considers why people are consuming twice the amount of opioids as 20 years ago, and asks the crucial question - do they actually work?(n)

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents action from the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the second round of the open singles and the semi-finals of the ladies singles. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam explores the rich culture of Scotland, before exploring the history of deaf education in Scotland. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Life, Death and Laughter
programme.desc: The Big Yin returns to Glasgow's famous King's Theatre, where his journey into comedy first began, to talk life, death and laughter, in a far-reaching interview with BBC Arts Editor Will Gompertz. It's a conversation that looks back on the comic's past and ahead to the future, covering all elements of his unusually packed existence - how he got started, his approach to comedy, his Scottish roots, and how Parkinson's disease is the latest thing he's having to laugh at(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore look for bargains in Scotland, where they enjoy a very soggy Highland Games, before an auction back across the border in Clitheroe(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hugh tries to sell his families award winning cheese at farmers markets in Bath, while the Kimber family sell their meat and dairy products straight off the farm(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: There are more new arrivals at the home, meaning Jimmy and Jenny Desmond now have 28 rescued chimpanzees to look after, including an eight-week-old baby called Gaia. Plans are coming together for the new sanctuary in the forest, and the orphans learn to master tools so they can forage for food. But a deadly cold breaks out among the babies, with disastrous consequences. The team also rescues an adult male chimp called Jonny who brings a fresh set of challenges. The pressure is on to move the chimps to their new home before things get out of hand(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the ladies singles final. Julie Forrest of Scotland prevailed against Guernsey's 2011 champion Alison Merrien in last year's final. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Compilation edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and offering some new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits Rodmarton Manor, an extraordinary Arts and Crafts house in the Cotswolds(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey through eastern England takes him to the Snape Maltings music and arts venue, the Norfolk seaside resort of Great Yarmouth and the city of Norwich(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Shappi Khorsandi and Konnie Huq search the antiques shops of Sussex in the hope of making a profit, with expert guidance from Tim Medhust and Charles Hanson. Charles is determined to impress Shappi but a fishy buy could see them flounder at the north London auction, while Konnie is put through her paces with some tough haggling for an Arts and Crafts oak armchair(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: New arrivals keep arriving at the chimpanzee orphanage in Liberia, bringing Jimmy and Jenny Desmond and their team to breaking point as they find themselves providing around-the-clock care for 38 rescued babies. After losing one of the chimps to illness, they are more determined than ever to build them a sanctuary in the forest where they can live like wild chimps. To prepare for the move, the orphans are learning to make their own nests and get their first taste of life in the forest(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP heads to Europe to discover how the divisions exposed by Brexit are reflected across the EU, exploring the role that national identity and pride have had to play in fuelling the rise of right-wing populism across the continent. He begins in the Netherlands, and the fishing towns of Den Helder and Scheveningen, where he meets a veteran fisherman who explains how EU rules and regulations have made the job harder(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: In the finale series, Cathy and her family decamp to the English countryside to celebrate Derek's birthday. This time, the family are recovering from a party the night before, and as Cathy nurses a hangover, she learns that she and Michael were less than discreet in front of the others. Subtle comedy, starring Lesley Manville and Peter Mullan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Chris Packham: 7.7 Billion People and Counting
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates the impact of a growing human population on the world in light of predictions that the number of humans could reach 10 billion by the year 2050. He considers the steps that will need to be taken for Earth's natural resources to support so many people, visiting a city in Brazil that is on the verge of running out of water and a community school in Nigeria that may present a possible solution. Featuring contributions from leading environmental experts including David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Northern Ireland, where they find out about the invention of the ejector seat, and visit Armagh Observatory, a railway museum located inside a barber shop, and SS Nomadic, known as Titanic's little sister(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room(n)

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?(n)

programme.title: The Prosecutors
programme.desc: The prosecutors deal with two violent domestic murders of women, who were killed by someone they knew, and the victim of an assault, Viv Driver-Hart, who is unhappy that her attacker has not been prosecuted. She has written to the CPS to appeal their decision under an new initiative, the Victims' Right to Review(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank charts the rise and fall of high-rise flats by following the history of one estate - the 19-storey Lincoln in Bow, east London, designed in 1960(n)

programme.title: Chinese New Year: The Biggest Celebration on Earth
programme.desc: Documentary exploring new year celebrations in China, through the personal stories of people involved in traditional celebrations, as well as those whose livelihoods are dependent on the holiday. Kate Humble, Ant Anstead and Jing Lusi attend the Harbin Ice and Snow Festival, where people celebrate in temperatures 30 degrees below zero, and experience rush hour in Beijing, as people struggle to make it home to their families. Plus, the Hairy Bikers join a flotilla of motorbikes in Guangzhou province(n)

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the America of small towns and trailer parks, discovering how these much-maligned territories had an immensely beneficial impact on American art. From the small-town brilliance of Grant Wood, to the small-town alienation of Edward Hopper, to the spooky Dust Bowl symbolism of Alexandre Hogue, interstitial America inspired much that was great. The film culminates in the assemblages of David Smith, the leading sculptor of Abstract Expressionism(n)

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: James reassembles the past to hear what it sounded like, piecing together the 195 parts of the game-changing 1963 Dansette Bermuda portable record player. The presenter also reminisces about the musical revolution these players inspired(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: A journey along the American river, whose fingers stretch into nearly half of the USA, collecting water from 31 states. The documentary follows the Mississippi through varied landscapes from its source to its mouth, starting in the towering Rocky Mountains of Wyoming and Montana, where billions of tons of water, ultimately destined to flow south are trapped as ice and snow(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley and a team of scientists investigate some of the easiest and most effective ways to get the biggest benefits of exercise, and turning what we think we know about fitness on its head. The team reveal why walking 10,000 steps a day is just a marketing ploy, and that two minutes of intense exercise is all you need per week. Elsewhere in the programme, they also discuss ways to help you stick to your fitness plan, and discuss exercises that can actually have a positive impact on brain activity(n)

programme.title: The Prosecutors
programme.desc: Prosecutors in the Complex Casework units of CPS Mersey-Cheshire and CPS South-East prepare for trials in separate historical cases. In 1993, 16-year-old Claire Tiltman was killed in an alleyway, but without direct evidence, the main suspect, Colin Ash-Smith, was not charged with her murder. Prosecutor Nigel Pilkington tries to build a circumstantial case against him. Meanwhile, in Mersey-Cheshire, a non-recent sex abuse case is being prepared for trial, with Keith Cavendish Coulson facing 42 counts of indecent assault(n)

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke concludes the documentary by revealing how the decline of the Egyptian Empire ran parallel with a creative revival that produced, among other proclamations of the pharaoh's power, colossal statues of Ramses II. He then discovers that invasions by foreign powers such as the Nubians and the Romans led to the production of hybrid forms of art, before revealing a selection of satirical work that continues to inspire Egypt's graffiti artists today(n)

programme.title: Chinese New Year: The Biggest Celebration on Earth
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King experience life in Beijing on New Year's Eve, including finding out how the Zhangs are preparing for the biggest family get-together of the year when the whole city comes to a halt as everyone gathers round to tuck into the traditional reunion dinner. Jing Lusi goes behind the scenes on CCTV's entertainment show Chunwan Gala, which is watched by 800 million viewers, Kate Humble takes part in a huge flower auction in Yunnan province, and Ant Anstead discovers how baijiu is made in 450-year-old pits and learns the subtle etiquette that surrounds the drinking of the spirit(n)

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: The artistic legacy of the Middle Ages, when carvers created some of the most significant pieces of British craftwork, including the roof of Westminster Hall and the Coronation Chair, under the sponsorship of the monarchy and the Church. However, The Reformation saw many wooden wonders disappear for good. Narrated by Paul Copley(n)

programme.title: Robert Rauschenberg: Pop Art Pioneer
programme.desc: Documentary profiling the work of Robert Rauschenberg, the influential American artist who was the first to win the Golden Lion at the Venice Biennale in 1963, and is said to have blazed a trail for creatives in the US throughout the 20th century. Rauschenberg's work has rarely been seen in the UK, but the Tate Modern is about to change that via a major new retrospective due to open this month. Ahead of the exhibition, Alastair Sooke travels to the east coast of the USA to talk to some of the people closest to Rauschenberg. His interviews provide a portrait of a man with boundless curiosity and a restlessness for experimentation, whose 60-year-long career saw him bridge the gap between the abstract expressionists of the 1950s, and the pop-art enthusiasts of the 1960s(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: A journey along the American river, whose fingers stretch into nearly half of the USA, collecting water from 31 states. The documentary follows the Mississippi through varied landscapes from its source to its mouth, starting in the towering Rocky Mountains of Wyoming and Montana, where billions of tons of water, ultimately destined to flow south are trapped as ice and snow(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: Following the journey of three young cheetah siblings as they set out across the savannah in Namibia, with specially designed on-board cameras monitoring their progress. As they move through the acacia woodland, thorny scrub and open grassy plains they face encounters with animals such as zebras and warthogs that allow them to test their hunting skills(n)

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: The documentary follows British wildlife throughout summer, when animals flock from afar and competition for food is high. Hunters time their arrival from Africa to feast on the huge glut of flying insects, falcons chase after dragonflies, and sea urchins are forced to protect themselves from harmful UV rays. Plus, thermal cameras reveal the activities of nightjars on a Dorset heath and thunderstorms bring out hordes of hungry snails. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Concorde: A Supersonic Story
programme.desc: As new £19million museum Aerospace Bristol prepares to open, this documentary takes a look at the life of the most glamorous plane ever built. Rare footage tells the forgotten row between the French and British governments over the name of Concorde, which threatened to derail the whole project. Engineers, flight technicians and frequent fliers tell the supersonic story, aided by politician Michael Heseltine and actress Joan Collins, while a former passenger recalls sharing an intimate moment with the Rolling Stones. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of a famously lavish five-day party held by the Shah of Iran in 1971 to celebrate 2,500 years of Persian monarchy. The event was held at a specially constructed oasis surrounded by a city of tents and attended by 60 world leaders, who enjoyed a five-course banquet and a pageant featuring thousands of soldiers dressed in historical costume. However, the decadence on display had far-reaching consequences, breeding resentment of the Shah's regime among the Iranian people(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nadiya Hussain takes a trip to the Himalayan country of Nepal to learn more about its people, places and food as part of her adventure to explore her identity. She begins in Kathmandu, where she joins a supper club run by young foodies who share with her the vastly varied cuisine of their country, then heads to the historic city of Bhaktapur, which was devastated by the earthquake of 2015, and visits an abbey to meet nuns from a Buddhist sect who practise kung fu and teach female empowerment(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate whether the safety of products and appliances in homes can be trusted, in the wake of recent high-profile recalls(n)

programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a cemetery lodge in the Cumbria town of Maryport and a house in Neath Port Talbot, and Dion Dublin casts his eye over a commercial unit in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Kelly family from Aberdeen spend a trial week living in New Zealand, but the idea of leaving their loving family behind proves difficult(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: The comedian and Mandy Starr head to Neath Working Men's Social Club to discover the pulling power of tribute acts inspired by stars from Tina Turner to Tom Jones. They meet two sisters who have been coming to the club weekly for half a century, and Owen has a lesson in bingo etiquette(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.desc: Bear has a run in with a woman who has built a business around waiting. Meanwhile, Jimmi starts to obsess about his father(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Poor weather plagues Bunty and Mrs McCarthy's picnic. Meanwhile, Father Brown must intervene when it appears that a dark spirit has murderous intent(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple look for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £450,000, and also visits the Royal Crown Derby factory to find out about the porcelain industry(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hugh tries to sell his family's award winning cheese at farmers markets in Bath, while the Kimber family sell their meat and dairy products straight off the farm(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore look for bargains in Scotland, where they enjoy a very soggy Highland Games, before an auction back across the border in Clitheroe(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern reveal some of the easy fixes that can help to dramatically improve people's diet, fitness and mental wellbeing. In the first edition, Hugh and Steph challenge one GPs' surgery to test out solutions for their compulsive snacking. They also tackle their own health concerns, from Hugh's stress to Steph's sensitive stomach(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the midweek Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v West Ham United at King Power Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford. Leicester have enjoyed an excellent campaign so far, but the Hammers will have been looking to demonstrate how much they have improved under David Moyes. United have endured an inconsistent campaign and the Clarets will have hoped for a first win here since 1962. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Norwich City, Chelsea v Arsenal, Aston Villa v Watford, Bournemouth v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Southampton, Everton v Newcastle United and Sheffield United v Manchester City(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with a woman who planned to move to New Zealand following a painful divorce, discovering if she persuaded her second husband to join her in starting over(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: Mandy Starr and Owen visit the Newbridge Memo, exploring the 1920s art deco theatre and the ballroom where young locals in the 1940s and 1950s would come to see dance bands. They talk to a couple who met at the venue half a century ago and still attend dances there today(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt are in Lincoln to help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Sheffield, while host Eric Knowles indulges his passion for vintage cars(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie and David's bond grows, while Jimmi receives a visit from his father. Emma finds herself trapped between two feuding brothers - and their cockroaches(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing clergyman uncovers secrets, hypocrisy and murder at a finishing school for young ladies. Mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £550,000 budget look for a property in Dorset, and visits the oyster beds of Poole harbour to learn how the shellfish are farmed sustainably(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth continue their profit-making challenge along the highways and byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Along the way, they visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy more pastry products, before heading off to auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells are thrown into panic as past events come back to haunt them and with Sharon having the upper hand - what lengths will she go to, to get revenge? As the election party gets under way, everyone is concerned by the sight of a drunken Linda, leaving Mick trying desperately to get her home. Lola urges Sheree to make amends with Patrick, but he is not prepared for what she tells him, and Kat opens up to Kush about her past(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: There is great excitement in Saint Marie when the Tour des Antilles cycling race comes to the island but the event turns sinister when the favourite, Xavier Prince, is found dead halfway around. All evidence initially points to Xavier having crashed on one of the most dangerous sections of the course, but Jack suspects it has more to do with the discovery of a piece of fabric torn from a cycling shirt. However, one of the riders is keeping a sensitive secret. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Stoke Newington, Hackney, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda is humiliated when Mick describes her actions the previous night - but their argument is cut short by shocking news from Ollie's school. As if that were not enough for the troubled landlady, she also faces Sharon, who wants to know why she said Keanu was alive. Meanwhile, Lisa tells Phil she and Louise are moving to Portugal - a plan he opposes - Ian frantically tries to get his fellow residents to vote for him in the election, and Daniel and Jean run rings around Rainie and Stuart(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Tension mounts between Caitlin and Joe before they dramatically clash over Ruby, while Dylan is under pressure to cater for Scarlett's community Burns Supper event, and Angus is furious to discover Bob has left town without telling Kim(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. The investigation into Jenny's murder grinds to a halt when it emerges that she was dead before her body was left on the tracks, but the women at the refuge demand answers. Thomas makes some friends in high places who offer to help him achieve his political ambitions - but there is a price to be paid for their support(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Serena needs to avoid causing trouble with an inspection looming, but when Jason has problems with the privatised porter company, she must decide if she can afford to help him. Nicky finds herself having to treat her own mother, who has been involved in a road accident, and is forced to take desperate measures to help her. Essie realises that she has to tell Ben everything about the illegal adoption if they are to stay together, but is not sure she can trust him with the truth(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: The group sets up a stall at a local farmers' market, offering visitors a taste of vegan food - as well as a toy dog on a barbecue smothered in fake blood. programme.desc: The Kelly family from Aberdeen spend a trial week living in New Zealand, but the idea of leaving their loving family behind proves difficult(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A customer thrown out of a bar takes revenge by driving into a group of locals outside, and the owner of a restaurant catches a group planning to leave without paying(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear has a run in with a woman who has built a business around waiting. Meanwhile, Jimmi starts to obsess about his father(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Poor weather plagues Bunty and Mrs McCarthy's picnic. Meanwhile, Father Brown must intervene when it appears that a dark spirit has murderous intent(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple look for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £450,000, and also visits the Royal Crown Derby factory to find out about the porcelain industry(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hugh tries to sell his family's award winning cheese at farmers markets in Bath, while the Kimber family sell their meat and dairy products straight off the farm(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore look for bargains in Scotland, where they enjoy a very soggy Highland Games, before an auction back across the border in Clitheroe(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern reveal some of the easy fixes that can help to dramatically improve people's diet, fitness and mental wellbeing. In the first edition, Hugh and Steph challenge one GPs' surgery to test out solutions for their compulsive snacking. They also tackle their own health concerns, from Hugh's stress to Steph's sensitive stomach(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the midweek Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v West Ham United at King Power Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford. Leicester have enjoyed an excellent campaign so far, but the Hammers will have been looking to demonstrate how much they have improved under David Moyes. United have endured an inconsistent campaign and the Clarets will have hoped for a first win here since 1962. Plus, Tottenham Hotspur v Norwich City, Chelsea v Arsenal, Aston Villa v Watford, Bournemouth v Brighton & Hove Albion, Crystal Palace v Southampton, Everton v Newcastle United and Sheffield United v Manchester City(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville investigate the quality of mobile phone coverage and hear about home owners stuck with mortgages they can barely afford(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a property in Stourbridge in the Midlands, a terraced house in Alkrington in Manchester and a semi-detached in Wigan in Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with a woman who planned to move to New Zealand following a painful divorce, discovering if she persuaded her second husband to join her in starting over(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: How a pickpocket prowling railway stations preying on women carrying backpacks and bags was hunted down. Plus, the thieves thwarted from taking a parcel from someone's doorstep(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt are in Lincoln to help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Sheffield, while host Eric Knowles indulges his passion for vintage cars(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie and David's bond grows, while Jimmi receives a visit from his father. Emma finds herself trapped between two feuding brothers - and their cockroaches(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing clergyman uncovers secrets, hypocrisy and murder at a finishing school for young ladies. Mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £550,000 budget look for a property in Dorset, and visits the oyster beds of Poole harbour to learn how the shellfish are farmed sustainably(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth continue their profit-making challenge along the highways and byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Along the way, they visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy more pastry products, before heading off to auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells are thrown into panic as past events come back to haunt them and with Sharon having the upper hand - what lengths will she go to, to get revenge? As the election party gets under way, everyone is concerned by the sight of a drunken Linda, leaving Mick trying desperately to get her home. Lola urges Sheree to make amends with Patrick, but he is not prepared for what she tells him, and Kat opens up to Kush about her past(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: There is great excitement in Saint Marie when the Tour des Antilles cycling race comes to the island but the event turns sinister when the favourite, Xavier Prince, is found dead halfway around. All evidence initially points to Xavier having crashed on one of the most dangerous sections of the course, but Jack suspects it has more to do with the discovery of a piece of fabric torn from a cycling shirt. However, one of the riders is keeping a sensitive secret. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia(n)

programme.desc: John Carmichael reads An Slaodaire Cadalach, written and illustrated by Frann Preston-Gannon and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald(n)

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: Stoick, Hiccup and Snotlout search for Spitelout when he goes missing, and ultimately find him on a distant island battling a fiery Singetail dragon(n)

programme.title: Buntàta Amh (Big Tatties)
programme.desc: The potato learns about the importance of flossing(n)

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Darren from Harris tells us about his favourite hobbies which including badminton and playing the saxophone(n)

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: When the toilets of the world rise up in 'revolootion', the crimebusting rodent and sidekick and Penfold take to the sewers of Tokyo to battle the evil Dr Loocifer(n)

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Super villain the Princess determines to 'pinkify' the world, forcing the hero rodent to dress as a little girl to stop her. Revival of the classic cartoon(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Mairi MacRitchie and skilful home bakers share their favourite seasonal fruit recipes. The presenter learns how the classic toffee apple flavour can be adapted for a fruity loaf with soft pieces of apple throughout and a sticky caramel icing(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on the relationship between film director Alfred Hitchcock and actress Ingrid Bergman(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports(n)

programme.title: Two Go to Tolsta - Sgeulachd Syd Agus Millie
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Millie and Syd, two children from Hertfordshire who ran away to their grandparents' in 1949(n)

programme.title: Sar Sgeoil
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald explores the locations, folklore and history that have inspired Diana Gabaldon's hugely successful Outlander series of novels(n)

programme.title: Mach à Seo
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon escapes city life for the solitude and scenery of the island of Rum(n)

programme.title: Concorde
programme.desc: Part one of two. The makers, pilots and passengers tell the story of how the Concorde saw off competition from the US and USSR to build the world's only supersonic airliner(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean visits Spar Cave on the Isle of Skye, finding that even reaching his destination proves tricky(n)

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: Amy Macdonald sings Down by the Water(n)

programme.title: Fonn Fonn Fonn
programme.desc: Offbeat music trivia quiz show, hosted by Gilleasbuig Ferguson, and team captains Carina MacLeod and Roddy Munro which invites guests to test their musical knowledge and kazoo-playing skills of guests Tormod Macleod, Michelle Macdonald, Marie Matheson and Sean Macleod(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps people in Wolverhampton sort the treasures from their trash, including a miners' lamp, a Scottish drinking vessel, and a lot of gold items(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends from Dundee answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Vicky, Dexter, Francesca and Dave, who have never met before, pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: During the opening day of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits, Jai is stressed and exhausted, while Laurel is left horrified(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Tens of thousands of Newcastle United fans pass through the Metro for a home game on the first day of the new football season - and many of them are far from pleased with the club's owner. A guide horse undergoes training on the Metro system before moving to London, and in Sunderland, a crackdown on fare dodgers gets underway(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A touring dance extravaganza comes to Midsomer, but nobody sees the vicious rivalries and long-running feuds that are brewing behind the scenes - at least not until they erupt into murder. Barnaby's investigation into the killing of a dancer is complicated by an unexpected visit from his father, while the sponsor of the event is at the centre of a conflict of his own as he tries to decide who should inherit his fortune. Guest starring Nigel Havers and Christopher Timothy, as well as former Strictly Come Dancing stars Cassidy Little, Faye Tozer, Danny Mac, Natalie Gumede and Tom Chambers(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: The Home Game
programme.desc: Retirees from Padstow blow a big budget on an oak porch for their renovation, and a property transformation in Liverpool uncovers a slice of history. Elsewhere, the development of a loft conversion for a family living in North London does not go according to plan(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: During the opening day of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits, Charity and Vanessa have plans, while Ryan makes a decision(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone and Fiz are horrified to discover the back door unlocked and Hope gone. Tim's shocked to find Charlie on his doorstep, and she tells him he can forget about the divorce as she cannot afford the legal fees. Bethany is gutted when Alya reveals that Ray has made her the bistro's new manager(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, Bradley and Barney visit the Red River Gorge in Kentucky, before heading to the supposedly haunted Poasttown Elementary School in Middletown, Ohio. The next day, they travel to Louisville, birthplace of legendary boxer Muhammad Ali, where they follow in his footsteps by sparring at the local boxing gym. At the end of a long day, Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned meal at a KFC restaurant in Kentucky - the home state of the chain's founder Colonel Harland Sanders(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz shows the police the passports and ferry tickets, convinced Jade has abducted Hope. Carla tells an appalled Bethany how Ray made Michelle sign a gagging order, and Charlie returns to No 4 with her bags and explains she's been evicted(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: After a vital discovery at the farm, Stan and Mick feel closer to shoring up their own suspicions about Jeremy. The arrival of Brett, a friend from New Zealand (played by Game of Thrones star Alfie Allen), increases tensions between Jeremy and Julie, while Ann grows ever more alarmed and suspicious of her cousin Jeremy's behaviour, and his handling of the family estate. As preparations for the inquest continue, Stan is concerned that if the bodies are released for cremation, vital evidence will be lost for ever(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends calling themselves Izzet Izzet Wicked answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Game show, hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Through the Keyhole
programme.desc: Actor and entertainer Shane Richie, Countdown's resident number-cruncher Rachel Riley, and Blur bassist Alex James form the panel this week, as Keith Lemon infiltrates another three mystery celebrities' homes. As ever, the host will be pointing out a selection of tell-tale possessions that may - or may not - hint at the identity of each property's occupant, and the three stars in the studio must guess 'who habitates in a house like this'(n)

programme.title: This Morning

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Solihull sort the treasures from their trash. Henry Nicholls tries to get his hands on a military watch and Fay Rutter cannot believe her luck when she is faced with 35 sovereign coins. But how much is she willing to pay? Jan Keyne gets a lesson in profit-making and Simon Schneider gets an offer he can refuse(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends calling themselves the Science Alliance answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Game show, hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Craig, Michala, Harry and Frances pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: As more drama unfolds during the opening day of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits, Andrea makes a discovery, and Belle is left confused(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Helen Skelton presents a report on the tourism industry, asking how easy it will be to get a bargain holiday in 2020 in the aftermath of the Thomas Cook collapse(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the opening day of Hawksford Outdoor Pursuits, and while Rhona surprises Marlon, he is left determined(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: At a time of year when many people feel the pinch in both their wallets and their waistlines, the consumer health show returns, presented by Sian Williams and Ranj Singh. Over the series they compare 12 of the latest diets, beginning with the 16:8 intermittent fasting diet and a meal replacement shake plan. They also find out how it's possible to have a drink ruining blowing a diet, investigating some of the latest low-calorie and low-alcohol tipples on the market(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. The first episode focuses on the conflict between love and duty that faces the royals - and which has occasionally threatened to bring down the House of Windsor. Edward VIII fatefully chose love when he abdicated to marry divorcee Wallis Simpson. Charles chose duty when he married Diana. The Queen, however, has managed to find a balance, maintaining a long marriage while becoming the longest-serving monarch(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star(n)

programme.desc: Five friends calling themselves the Science Alliance answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Game show, hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team travel to Walsall to surprise young widow Nina Perrin Seedhouse. With three children under seven to look after, she does not have time for anything else, so her garden is a blank canvas. It's up to the green-fingered experts to finally realise the dream garden her husband was going to create for his family(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall helps Elizabeth Wells and her daughter Janice track down the son Elizabeth put up for adoption 50 years earlier. She also offers expert assistance to Tracey Collins, who discovered at the age of 17 that she has a brother, Thomas, who she has never met. Tracey's father had vowed to help her find him, but died before he was able to share crucial information about his location. Now, Tracey seeks guidance from Nicky Campbell in tracking Thomas down(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: The hunt for Sian O'Callaghan continues as Det Supt Steve Fulcher and his team see suspects come and go with no leads to follow. Local woman Karen Edwards notices that she only lives a few doors away from the missing girl's mother, Elaine Pickford, but is only reminded of her own problems, having not seen her daughter Becky for several years. With things looking bleak for Wiltshire police, a brilliant piece of police work finally points Fulcher towards a definite suspect. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman(n)

programme.desc: Former EastEnder and The Bill bad cop Todd Carty guest stars in this festive special of the rural drama. He plays an old army pal of Lord Ashfordly who moves into the hall while the peer is sunning himself in Bermuda, claiming he's there to keep an eye on the place after rumours of a planned break-in. However, he arouses the police's suspicions when he becomes involved with a teenage tearaway who seems to think Christmas isn't so much about giving and receiving as taking without the owner's permission.
